text: The aim of this article is to evaluate the general approach to a Christian interpretation of the physical world as understood by Keith Ward and presumably many others in the same way as he. It will appear that Ward and others attempt to support a theologically satisfactory accommodation of what in scientific terms is a physical world, as well as a spiritual world by using panentheistic criteria. This evaluation will necessarily follow a general reformational theological understanding. Some suggestions will be given on the reasons for the pervasiveness of panentheism as a school of thought amongst Christians today as well as the pertinence of a bipolar understanding of God amongst many members of the church. It is hoped that those who feel seduced by the concept of an inclusive generic Christian panentheism in one of the many possible forms will find some practical alternatives for a meaningful interpretation of God when such concepts are considered seriously. Die doel van hierdie artikel is om die algemene benadering tot ’n Christelike interpretasie van die werklikheid, soos algemeen toegepas deur Keith Ward, te evalueer. Dit word voorgestel dat hy panenteïstiese kriteria toepas in sy pogings om teologiese akkommodasie van ’n materiële en geestelike interpretasie van die werklikheid te steun. Hierdie evaluering volg ’n algemene reformatoriese teologiese perspektief. Voorstelle word gemaak om te verduidelik waarom panenteïsme so ’n impak op sommige Christene het dat hulle ’n bipolêre benadering tot God verkies. Verder word aanbevelings gemaak vir diegene wat ’n Christelike generiese panenteïsme in verskillende vorme aanloklik genoeg vind om dit sodoende als ’n alternatiewe interpretasie vir die tradisionele siening van God te oorweeg.
explanation: The passage reads like a polished academic abstract with domain-specific terminology, which AI-generated text can produce; the bilingual English-Afrikaans section is unusual for a single-authored piece and could suggest translation or AI assembly.
